Interface ProofTreeListener

All Known Implementing Classes:
CachingPruneHandler, DependencyTracker, ProofTreeAdapter, SlicingLeftPanel
public interface ProofTreeListener

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default void
    notesChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
    If the notes of a NodeInfo of a proof tree Node have changed.
    default void
    proofClosed(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The proof trees has been closed (the list of goals is empty).
    default void
    proofExpanded(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent has changed, and/or there are new descendants of that node.
    default void
    proofGoalRemoved(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The goal mentioned in the ProofEvent has been removed from the list of goals.
    default void
    proofGoalsAdded(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The goals mentiones in the list of added goals in the proof event have been added to the proof
    default void
    proofGoalsChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The goals mentiones in the list of added goals in the proof event have been added to the proof
    default void
    proofIsBeingPruned(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The proof tree under the node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent is in pruning phase.
    default void
    proofPruned(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The proof tree has been pruned under the node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent.
    default void
    proofStructureChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
    The structure of the proof has changed radically.

  • Method Details

    • proofExpanded

      default void proofExpanded(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent has changed, and/or there are new descendants of that node. Any nodes that are not descendants of that node are unaffected.

    • proofIsBeingPruned

      default void proofIsBeingPruned(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The proof tree under the node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent is in pruning phase. The subtree of node will be removed after this call but at this point the subtree can still be traversed (e.g. in order to free the nodes in caches). The method proofPruned is called, when the nodes are disconnect from node.
      Parameters:
      e - proof tree event specifying the node to be pruned

    • proofPruned

      default void proofPruned(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The proof tree has been pruned under the node mentioned in the ProofTreeEvent. In other words, that node should no longer have any children now. Any nodes that were not descendants of that node are unaffected.
      Parameters:
      e - proof tree event specifying the pruned node

    • proofStructureChanged

      default void proofStructureChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The structure of the proof has changed radically. Any client should rescan the whole proof tree.

    • proofClosed

      default void proofClosed(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The proof trees has been closed (the list of goals is empty).

    • proofGoalRemoved

      default void proofGoalRemoved(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The goal mentioned in the ProofEvent has been removed from the list of goals.

    • proofGoalsAdded

      default void proofGoalsAdded(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The goals mentiones in the list of added goals in the proof event have been added to the proof

    • proofGoalsChanged

      default void proofGoalsChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
      The goals mentiones in the list of added goals in the proof event have been added to the proof

    • notesChanged

      default void notesChanged(ProofTreeEvent e)
      If the notes of a NodeInfo of a proof tree Node have changed.
      Parameters:
      e - The ProofTreeEvent.
